mnemonics: RECURSE: Recursive Equation Construction Requires Understanding Steps Exactly.
Master Theorem: F(n) = aF(n/b) + f(n) - A Big Function Needs Breaking.
T(n) Tree: Trace The Recursive Tree To Tackle Time.
keyPoints: The recurrence relation is the fundamental equation that defines the algorithm's time complexity.
The three main methods to solve recurrences are: Substitution, Recursion Tree, and Master Theorem.
The Master Theorem provides a direct solution for recurrences of the form T(n) = aT(n/b) + f(n).
commonMistakes: Forgetting to add the cost of combining subproblem solutions in the recurrence.
Incorrectly applying the Master Theorem to recurrences that do not fit its standard form (e.g., T(n) = aT(n - b) + f(n)).
Making algebraic errors when solving the recurrence using the iteration method or induction.
